title: Verdict
description: One blind verifier's judgement of one task on one lens. Emitted by the verify panel (devflow.js mode:verify). Its unanimity across the panel is the merge gate that replaces the human reviewer, so a verdict must carry replayable evidence — evidence is required and non-empty. Default to pass:false whenever anything is unproven.

task.description: Id of the task being judged.

lens.description: Which independent lens this verdict represents: correctness (blind verifier rebuilds + runs the suite), completeness (intent<->diff coverage, no silent scope drop), runtime (web target driven through the real UI via chrome-devtools).

pass.description: true only if acceptance is OBJECTIVELY met on this lens. Unproven => false.

reason.description: Short justification of the pass/fail decision.

evidence.description: Replayable proof: commands run + observed output, file:line references, probe responses. At least one item required — a verdict with no evidence cannot gate a merge.
